description:
This conceptual diagram illustrates the decomposition of ground displacement into its vertical and horizontal components using satellite Line-Of-Sight (LOS) measurements from both ascending and descending passes.
The diagram shows a curved grey ground surface with a red reference point on it. Two satellites are depicted above the surface, associated with "LOS ascending" (from top-left) and "LOS descending" (from top-right).
Originating from the red reference point are several vectors:
*   `d_real` (green arrow) represents the true 3D ground displacement vector.
*   `d_LOS Asc` (red arrow) represents the component of displacement measured along the ascending Line-Of-Sight.
*   `d_LOS Desc` (blue arrow) represents the component of displacement measured along the descending Line-Of-Sight.
*   `d_v` (black arrow, pointing downwards) represents the vertical component of displacement.
*   `d_WE` (black dashed arrow, pointing right) represents the East-West component of displacement.
The diagram visually explains how the real 3D ground motion (`d_real`) can be resolved into vertical (`d_v`) and East-West (`d_WE`) components using measurements from two distinct satellite viewing geometries (ascending and descending Line-Of-Sight observations), which correspond to the `d_LOS Asc` and `d_LOS Desc` vectors.
